title: 7 Herbal Creams For Gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal creams for gastritis can provide relief from the discomforts of this condition. Gastritis is inflammation of the stomach lining, often caused by bacteria, stress, or certain foods. Herbal teas, made from specific herbs, can help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and pain.  One example is Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, which contains a powerful compound called curcumin. Curcumin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce stomach inflammation and promote healing. When consumed as a tea, curcumin can also help reduce symptoms of gastritis, such as bloating and nausea.  Another herb that can help is Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease stomach pain and reduce nausea. It can also help stimulate digestion and reduce bloating, making it a popular remedy for gastritis.  Licorice root, or Glycyrrhiza glabra, is another herb that can provide relief from gastritis symptoms. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. Licorice root can also help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid, reducing the risk of ulcers.  Using herbal teas for gastritis can bring several benefits to your life. They are a natural and non-invasive way to manage symptoms, without the risk of side effects associated with over-the-counter medications. Herbal teas can also help promote digestion and reduce bloating, making them a great addition to a healthy diet. Additionally, herbal teas can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can also contribute to overall stomach health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a strong anti-inflammatory agent. Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can irritate the stomach lining and cause gastritis. The active constituents in Curcuma longa creams, including curcuminoids and polyphenols, help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Curcuma longa creams can help to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as stomach pain and nausea. The bioactive constituents in Curcuma longa creams also have a protective effect on the mucous lining of the stomach, helping to prevent further irritation and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add turmeric powder, shea butter, and olive oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Pour it into a heat-proof container or a silicone ice cube tray.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely. Once solid, you can use the Curcuma longa cream as needed to help soothe gastritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, a common cause of gastritis. The anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. Gingerols and shogaols also have antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of gingerols and shogaols in Zingiber officinale creams can help soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale root powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (like lavender or peppermint).
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth and combined.
In a separate bowl, mix the Zingiber officinale root powder and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Add the Zingiber officinale mixture to the melted oil mixture and stir well. Add the essential oil and stir again.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, use as a cream for gastritis relief. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining. Glycyrrhizin also helps protect the stomach from acid damage and promotes the healing of gastric ulcers. The anti-inflammatory and protective properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as stomach pain and nausea. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may also promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria, further reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in glycerin, Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers or tin cans and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, store the cream in a cool, dry place and apply a thin layer to affected areas as needed to help soothe gastritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains quinine, a compound that helps reduce inflammation in the stomach. Quinine also has astringent properties, which help to decrease acid production and tighten the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Another active constituent, cinchonine, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation caused by gastritis. The tannins present in Cinchona officinalis creams also contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of gastritis. Regular use of Cinchona officinalis creams may help to reduce the frequency and severity of gastritis episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of water, 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Boil 2 cups of water and add 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark. Let it steep for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture and let it cool.
Melt 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler. Stir until the beeswax is melted.
Add the cooled Cinchona officinalis infusion, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Cinchona officinalis cream for gastritis is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These components have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. The aloe-emodin in Aloe barbadensis creams has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing acid from causing damage and promoting healing. The antioxidants in the cream, such as vitamins A and C, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gastritis. The soothing properties of Aloe barbadensis creams can also help calm stomach acid production, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/4 cup aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 1 teaspoon vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals.
Add aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture reaches 120°F to 130°F. Add distilled water and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, add peppermint essential oil and mix well. Use as a cream for gastritis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains essential oils that are rich in compounds like anethole, limonene, and pinene. These active constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining, common symptoms of gastritis. Anethole, in particular, has been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and discomfort. The antispasmodic properties of limonene and pinene also help to ease cramps and pain associated with gastritis, while their antioxidant properties protect the stomach lining from damage. By soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation, Foeniculum vulgare creams can provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel seeds) and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water until it reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 to 3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Foeniculum vulgare cream in the refrigerator and use 1 tablespoon as needed to soothe gastritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents menthol, menthone, and limonene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining that causes gastritis. Menthol also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, calming down the stomach and reducing acid production. The antispasmodic properties of limonene help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with gastritis. Regular use of Mentha x piperita creams may help to protect the stomach lining and improve digestion, alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for gastritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of water.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Stir in peppermint essential oil and water.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed to help soothe gastritis.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gastritis from forming because it can soothe and calm the stomach. Certain herbs like peppermint and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues. This can help protect the stomach lining and prevent irritation that leads to gastritis. Regular herbal tea consumption may help keep the stomach healthy.
faq_safe: For people with gastritis, drinking herbal teas daily can be a bit tricky. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger can calm the stomach, but others like chamomile and licorice root might make symptoms worse. It's best to start with small amounts and see how your body reacts, then adjust your tea choice accordingly to find what works for you.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with gastritis symptoms after a few days. Some people feel better within 24 to 48 hours. As your body adjusts, you may notice relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. It may take longer, up to a week or two, for your digestive system to fully settle and heal.
faq_time: For gastritis, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This helps soothe your stomach before eating a meal. Avoid drinking herbal tea too close to bedtime, as it might disturb your sleep. A gentle cup in the morning or a midday pick-me-up can be very calming for your stomach.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gastritis. Some herbs like ginger and peppermint may increase bleeding risk when taken with blood thinners. Licorice root can raise blood pressure, which may worsen gastritis symptoms if you're already taking blood pressure medication. It's essential to be aware of these potential interactions.
faq_pregnant: If you have gastritis while pregnant, it's best to talk to your healthcare provider about herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, might help soothe stomach discomfort, but others can be irritating. It's also possible that certain herbs could affect your pregnancy or baby's health, so it's a good idea to be cautious and get advice from your provider.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding with gastritis, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can irritate your stomach or affect breast milk. Peppermint and ginger teas might be soothing, but others like chamomile and licorice root could cause problems. It's a good idea to talk to your body and see how you react to different teas.
title_2: Soothe Gastritis with 7 Herbal Creams: A Guide for Heavy Drinkers
